question: Based on Table 1.2-2 safety features, Table 5.2-10 LTOP setpoints, and Ch.05 text on P-T limits, is the NuScale overpressure protection strategy adequate?

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Class 1 standards, with specific classifications for connected piping, and confirms use of the 2013 ASME BPVC edition as required by regulatory guidance.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch01_0732.title: SRP 15.4.1, Rev 3: Uncontrolled Control Rod Assembly Withdrawal From a Subcritical or Low Power Startup ConditionII.1.A Thermal Margin Limits
nuscale_ch01_0732.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding uncontrolled control rod assembly withdrawal during startup. It notes that NuScale uses critical heat flux (CHF) instead of departure from nucleate boiling (DNBR) for thermal margin limits and applies a heat generation rate limit to ensure fuel centerline temperatures remain within safe bounds. Additionally, some criteria related to forced coolant flow are not applicable due to NuScale’s use of passive natural circulation.

nuscale_ch01_0118.title: 1.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
nuscale_ch01_0118.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the Combined License (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified plant portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet NRC-endorsed conditions as specified in relevant regulatory guides and federal regulations. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific code cases applied.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the methodology and regulatory compliance for establishing pressure-temperature operational limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes how the limits are calculated using ASME and NRC guidelines, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides the resulting curves and data to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified plant portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch01_0769.title: SRP 15.6.1, Rev 2: Inadvertent Opening of a PWR Pressurizer Relief Valve or a BWR Pressure Relief ValveAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0769.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 15.6.1, which addresses the inadvertent opening of pressurizer relief valves, because NuScale does not use power-operated relief valves (PORVs) that are susceptible to inadvertent operation. Instead, NuScale uses spring-loaded ASME code safety relief valves, and any mechanical failure of these valves is addressed by analyses of inadvertent ECCS valve actuation in another section. Thus, the guidance is only partially applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0160.title: DSRS 3.11, Rev. 0: Environmental Qualification of Mechanical and Electrical EquipmentII.25 Design and Procurement Specifications
nuscale_ch01_0160.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to the environmental qualification of mechanical and electrical equipment, as well as design and procurement specifications. It notes that certain requirements, such as DSRS 3.11 II.25, are not applicable to NuScale and are instead items for the COL (Combined License) applicant, while other standards (e.g., SRP 3.12 and 3.13) are met with no exceptions. Additionally, guidance specific to BWR plants is marked as not applicable to NuScale’s design.

nuscale_ch01_0113.title: Figure 1.7-3b: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0113.summary: This section provides the legend for Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It explains the standard symbols and abbreviations for valve positions, actuator types, and other control elements, clarifying their normal operating states and specific functionalities as depicted on the diagrams.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch01_0228.title: DSRS 7.2.8, Rev 0: Auxiliary FeaturesAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0228.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for auxiliary features and related systems, noting that for DSRS 7.2.8, there are no specific acceptance criteria, but conformance is indicated. The document also details compliance with relevant standards and guidance for setpoints, control of access, system interactions, multi-unit stations, controls, and monitoring, referencing applicable regulatory guides and technical reports.

nuscale_ch01_0227.title: DSRS 7.2.7, Rev 0: Setpoints II.3 Generic Letter (GL) 91-04
nuscale_ch01_0227.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for nuclear safety-related instrumentation setpoints and associated systems. It confirms that NuScale’s setpoint methodology aligns with NRC guidance, including Generic Letter 91-04, and references the relevant technical report. The section also notes conformance or partial conformance with additional regulatory guides and standards for auxiliary features, control access, system interactions, and monitoring requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions (AND, OR, NOT), bistable outputs, control room indicators, switch types, and other functional symbols, ensuring clear interpretation of system diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0767.title: DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System Malfunction That Increases Reactor Coolant InventoryII.4.B Input Parameters and Initial Conditions - Scram Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0767.summary: This section confirms that the NuScale design fully conforms to DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2 requirements regarding input parameters and initial conditions, including scram characteristics, for chemical and volume control system malfunctions that increase reactor coolant inventory. It also clarifies that certain SRP guidance related to pressurizer relief valves is not applicable to NuScale, as its design uses spring-loaded safety valves instead of power-operated relief valves, thus avoiding vulnerabilities to inadvertent operation.

nuscale_ch01_0112.title: Figure 1.7-3a: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0112.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It details the standard symbols and notations for various valves, fire and safety equipment, and specialty items, as well as conventions for vents, drains, and flow paths, ensuring consistent interpretation of P&ID drawings. Exceptions and specific system abbreviations are noted directly on the diagrams.

edges.description: Section A states that the NuScale design complies with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Section III requirements for reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity, which are regulatory prerequisites for the overpressure protection strategies evaluated in Section B's referenced SRP/DSRS criteria.

Section A establishes that the reactor coolant pressure boundary components comply with ASME Code requirements, while Section B defines which structures, systems, and components (SSCs) are part of the certified design subject to those requirements, thus connecting the scope of compliance in A to the design boundaries in B.

Section A states that the NuScale design complies with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B elaborates on the specific provisions and code cases used to ensure overpressure protection compliance, thus Section B provides details for Section A's general compliance statement.

Section A states that the reactor pressure vessel and associated components comply with ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B provides specific details on how certain attachments and welds on the reactor vessel meet those same ASME BPVC code provisions.

Section A establishes that the reactor coolant pressure boundary complies with ASME and regulatory code requirements, while Section B identifies which systems and components are included in the certified design and thus subject to those compliance standards, connecting the scope of compliance to the specific plant features relevant for overpressure protection.

Section A lists conformance with regulatory guidance on radiation protection design features, while Section B addresses conformance with thermal margin and safety system requirements for accident scenarios; both sections provide evidence supporting the adequacy of NuScale's safety strategies, including overpressure protection, by demonstrating compliance with relevant regulatory criteria.

Section A lists the NuScale FSAR's conformance with various regulatory guides on radiation protection design features, while Section B defines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design (including safety features) and site-specific elements, connecting them through the identification of which safety features (from Section A) are part of the certified design referenced in Section B.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements for radiation protection design features, while Section B provides technical details and code compliance for reactor vessel attachments, with both sections connected through their references to ASME BPVC code requirements for safety-related design and inspection.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements for radiation protection design features, some of which reference other sections and tables, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified NuScale design and site-specific COL items, with both sections connected through their reliance on referenced tables (e.g., Table 1.9-2, Table 1.8-1) to define compliance and interfaces.

Section A discusses conformance with regulatory criteria for safety systems and accident analyses, while Section B specifies the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified NuScale design (including safety features) and site-specific elements, connecting them through the identification of which safety features and systems (such as overpressure protection) are part of the certified design relevant to the adequacy analysis.

Section A discusses the thermal margin limits and safety system requirements that must be met during certain transients, while Section B outlines the regulatory compliance and code requirements for overpressure protection systems, connecting them through the need for the overpressure protection strategy to satisfy both the safety analysis limits and regulatory code compliance.

Section A outlines regulatory criteria and conformance for safety systems and thermal margin limits, while Section B provides detailed compliance information with ASME code requirements for reactor vessel attachments, connecting them through the shared focus on ensuring reactor vessel integrity under pressure-temperature limits relevant to overpressure protection adequacy.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and safety analysis requirements that the NuScale design must conform to, while Section B specifies the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and the site-specific elements as documented in the COL application, connecting them through the need to ensure that all safety features and regulatory criteria (from Section A) are properly addressed and integrated at the design interface points (from Section B).

Section A identifies the certified design boundaries and interfaces, which determine the scope of overpressure protection features discussed in Section B regarding compliance with ASME and NRC requirements.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and site-specific design, which determines which reactor vessel components and safety features (referenced in Section B) are governed by the certified design and thus subject to the overpressure protection strategy adequacy analysis.

Section A and Section B both address the interface requirements between the NuScale certified design and the site-specific design in the COL application, with Section B listing the specific Combined License Information Items referenced in Section A.

Section A outlines compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory requirements for overpressure protection, while Section B details how specific reactor vessel components and welds meet ASME code provisions, thus providing evidence that supports the adequacy of the overpressure protection strategy described in Section A.

Section A describes the overpressure protection provisions and code compliance for the reactor coolant boundary, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and identifies which systems (including overpressure protection features) are part of the certified design, thus connecting the scope of protection features in A to their regulatory boundary in B.

Section A provides technical details and compliance information about reactor vessel attachments and welds, which are part of the certified design components referenced in Section B's discussion of interface requirements between certified and site-specific designs.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that are cited as supporting documentation for the compliance claims made in Section B regarding adherence to regulatory codes and code cases.

Section A lists references that provide the technical and regulatory basis for the NuScale FSAR's discussion of reactor coolant boundary integrity, which supports the adequacy claims made in Section B regarding conformance with regulatory criteria for overpressure protection strategies.

Section A discusses conformance with regulatory criteria for overpressure events, while Section B specifies compliance with ASME and 10 CFR 50.55a code requirements for reactor coolant boundary integrity, thus Section A's analysis of overpressure protection strategies is intended to satisfy the regulatory and code compliance requirements detailed in Section B.

Section A and Section B both reference NuScale FSAR conformance with regulatory criteria for different accident scenarios, providing evidence that the design's safety features and system responses (such as overpressure protection and thermal margin limits) collectively satisfy the regulatory requirements relevant to the adequacy of the overpressure protection strategy.

Section A references the regulatory requirements and standards (such as ASME Code and SRP 3.12) that Section B claims the NuScale design complies with, specifically regarding the integrity and qualification of reactor coolant boundary components.

Section A specifies the design and procurement requirements for mechanical and electrical equipment qualification, which are prerequisites for the overpressure protection systems whose adequacy is evaluated in Section B's accident analyses.

Section A provides the legend and status conventions for valves as depicted in P&IDs, which are necessary for interpreting the system configurations that Section B asserts are compliant with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements for reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity.

Section A provides the legend and status conventions for valves as depicted in piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are referenced in Section B's regulatory review to verify that safety-related systems (such as overpressure protection) are configured and operate in accordance with regulatory requirements.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical basis for overpressure protection, while Section B documents NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for overpressure events, connecting them through the use of referenced standards to demonstrate regulatory compliance.

Section A provides references cited in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B lists regulatory criteria and their applicability or conformance status in the FSAR, with Section A's references supporting the claims of regulatory conformance or applicability described in Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines cited in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B presents diagram legends used to interpret piping and instrumentation diagrams referenced elsewhere in the FSAR; thus, Section A cites external documents that may inform the interpretation or application of the diagram conventions shown in Section B.

Section A addresses the conformance of NuScale's safety analysis with regulatory criteria for overpressure events, while Section B discusses the environmental qualification and design specifications for mechanical and electrical equipment, which are prerequisites for ensuring that overpressure protection systems function as intended.

Section A outlines regulatory requirements and conformance criteria for overpressure protection events, while Section B provides the P&ID legend necessary to interpret the valve status and control features referenced in the safety analysis, thus enabling verification that the design meets the regulatory expectations.

Section A outlines regulatory conformance and requirements for piping systems, while Section B provides the legend for interpreting valve positions and statuses on piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are necessary for verifying compliance with those requirements.

Section A describes the design features intended to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies that these features comply with regulatory codes and standards (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC), directly connecting the design implementation to regulatory compliance.

Section A describes the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which is a prerequisite for ensuring that radiation protection design features in Section B are effective, as loss of boundary integrity would compromise radiation protection.

Section A describes the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which is a prerequisite for meeting the regulatory criteria and thermal margin limits referenced in Section B.

Section A describes the setpoint methodology for safety-related instrumentation, confirming conformance with regulatory guidance, while Section B states compliance with ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a for reactor coolant boundary integrity, connecting them through the requirement that setpoint methodologies must align with code-based design and operational standards.

Section A describes the conformance of NuScale's setpoint methodology with regulatory guidance, which directly supports the adequacy of overpressure protection strategies discussed in Section B's analysis of thermal margin and safety system initiation requirements.

Section A describes the setpoint methodology for safety-related instrumentation and its conformance with regulatory guidance, while Section B states that the reactor coolant pressure boundary components comply with ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a, thus Section A's methodology supports Section B's claim of regulatory compliance by ensuring instrumentation setpoints are established according to required codes.

Section A describes the NuScale setpoint methodology's conformance with regulatory guidance (GL 91-04, RIS 2006-17) for safety-related instrumentation, which is directly relevant to Section B's requirements for thermal margin limits and safety system performance during overpressure events, thereby linking the adequacy of setpoints (A) to the fulfillment of overpressure protection requirements (B).

Section A describes the design features that maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B confirms that the setpoint methodology for safety-related instrumentation (which includes overpressure protection setpoints) conforms to regulatory requirements, thereby supporting the adequacy of the protection strategy.

Section A describes the requirement to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B confirms that the setpoint methodology for overpressure protection conforms to regulatory guidance (GL 91-04), thereby supporting the adequacy of the protection strategy for maintaining boundary integrity.

Section A references and summarizes the conformance of the NuScale setpoint methodology with regulatory criteria, specifically citing the applicability of Generic Letter 91-04 as detailed in Section B.

Section A provides the logic and symbol definitions for instrumentation and control systems, which are used to implement the safety functions required by the regulatory codes and standards referenced in Section B.

Section A provides the logic symbols and definitions used in instrumentation and controls diagrams, which are necessary for interpreting the design features and regulatory conformance discussed in Section B.

Section A provides the logic symbol definitions used in instrumentation and controls diagrams, which are necessary to interpret the logic and setpoint actions referenced in Section B's regulatory requirements for safety system actuation and conformance.

Section A describes the initial conditions and scram characteristics for a chemical and volume control system malfunction scenario, which are necessary input parameters for evaluating whether the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) design in Section B, compliant with ASME Code and 10 CFR 50.55a, can withstand such events as part of the overpressure protection strategy.

Section A specifies the input parameters and initial conditions for chemical and volume control system malfunctions, which are necessary prerequisites for the thermal margin limits and accident analyses described in Section B.

Section A provides diagrammatic symbols and conventions for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams, which are necessary for understanding the physical configuration and operation of overpressure protection features whose compliance with regulatory codes is asserted in Section B.

Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret piping and instrumentation diagrams, which is a prerequisite for understanding the specific system configurations and safety features referenced in Section B's regulatory criteria and conformance statements.

Section A provides the logic symbols and definitions used in instrumentation and controls diagrams, which are necessary for interpreting the control logic relevant to the overpressure protection strategies analyzed in Section B's regulatory conformance discussion.

Section A provides the logic symbols and definitions used for instrumentation and control diagrams, while Section B provides the legend for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagram (P&ID) symbols, so both sections are structurally related as they specify how to read and understand the technical diagrams referenced in the analysis.

Section A references the regulatory requirements and input parameters for overpressure events, while Section B provides the legend necessary to interpret the piping and instrumentation diagrams that depict the physical implementation of overpressure protection features, thus Section B supports the understanding and verification of the system described in Section A.
